Title: Allelic variations in GA20ox3 regulate fruit length and seed germination timing for high-altitude adaptation in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Sichuan ecotype; Tibet ecotype; Col-0; ga20ox3-1 background
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Bioactive GA biosynthetic branch leading to GA1 and GA4
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: GA precursors in the GA20-oxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GA20-oxidized intermediates that feed production of bioactive GA1 and GA4
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GA 20-oxidation steps in gibberellin biosynthesis, generating intermediates upstream of bioactive GA1 and GA4 formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for fruit length and seed germination timing in this study, but not explicitly established as universally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fruits (9 DAP); imbibed seeds; cold-stratified seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit elongation and seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GA20ox3 expression increases GA1/GA4 levels, promotes longer fruits, and reduces cold stratification requirement for germination; reduced expression causes shorter fruits and delayed germination
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stratification; paclobutrazol treatment; altitude-associated low temperature
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter haplotypes or editing of causal promoter SNPs could tune gibberellin levels, fruit length, and germination timing for climate adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced GA20ox3 expression in high-altitude haplotypes is associated with shorter fruits, fewer seeds per fruit, delayed germination, and likely a survival–fecundity trade-off
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing fruits and early germinating seeds; promoter activity increases after cold stratification; lower in Tibet ecotype than Sichuan ecotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increasing expression during fruit development; active in germinating seeds; lower in Tib than Sic; induced after cold stratification
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GA20ox3 promoters from Sichuan and Tibet ecotypes; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; bivariate GWAS; RT-qPCR; endogenous GA quantification by LC-ESI-MS/MS; promoter-GUS assays; overexpression; mutant analysis; complementation with promoter swap constructs; dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; genetic epistasis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing/SNP genotyping of RILs for GWAS (1,048,575 SNPs); promoter sequence dataset from Arabidopsis 1001 Genomes and additional accessions; figshare repository for SNP data; no accession number specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA20ox3 was identified by GWAS and expression analysis as the major gene jointly controlling fruit length and seed germination timing. 2. Natural promoter variation, especially around SNP-418 and nearby sites, altered GA20ox3 transcription without changing enzyme activity. 3. Reduced GA20ox3 expression lowered GA1/GA4 accumulation, causing shorter fruits and delayed germination in the high-altitude ecotype. 4. GA20ox3 is directly repressed by a DELLA-DOF6 complex, creating a negative feedback link between GA biosynthesis and GA signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coding-sequence variation between Sichuan and Tibet alleles caused one amino acid substitution but did not alter apparent enzyme function under 35S overexpression. Global promoter haplotypes differ in DOF6 binding affinity and associate with altitude/temperature and dormancy variation.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; Sichuan ecotype; Tibet ecotype; dof6 mutant; DOF6-OE
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression via promoter binding; repression strengthened by DELLA interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Gibberellin biosynthesis controlling fruit elongation and seed germination timing
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses GA20ox3 transcription by binding the DOF motif in the promoter P8 region near SNP-418
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Cold stratification/developmental cues; DELLA proteins in GA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in a negative feedback loop between GA biosynthesis and GA signaling through DELLA-DOF6 repression of GA20ox3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms repressive complexes with DELLA proteins GAI, RGA, RGL1, and RGL2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator that directly tunes GA20ox3 expression and thus GA1/GA4 levels, fruit length, and germination timing
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fruits; imbibed seeds; cold-stratified seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit elongation and seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DOF6 overexpression shortens fruits and delays germination by repressing GA20ox3; dof6 mutation increases germination and tends to increase fruit length
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stronger DOF6-mediated repression contributes to delayed germination and shorter fruits, consistent with alpine adaptation but potentially reduced fecundity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreases during fruit development; expressed during embryo maturation, highest in dry seeds, then declines during stratification
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inverse to GA20ox3 during fruit development; decreases with cold stratification
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in overexpression and FLAG constructs
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GA20ox3 promoter; GAI; RGA; RGL1; RGL2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 mutant; genetic interaction with ga20ox3-1; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing DOF6-binding motifs in the GA20ox3 promoter may be more precise than altering DOF6 itself for tuning germination timing and fruit traits
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression survey using ePlant database for DOF family; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DOF6 directly binds the GA20ox3 promoter near SNP-418 and represses transcription. 2. DOF6 overexpression causes shorter fruits and lower germination, whereas dof6 mutation has the opposite tendency. 3. Promoter alleles differ in DOF6 affinity, explaining ecotype-specific GA20ox3 expression and phenotypes. 4. DOF6 acts in a negative feedback loop with DELLA-mediated GA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known as a negative regulator of seed germination; in this study also linked to fruit elongation through GA20ox3.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; rgl2 mutant; della-q; ga20ox3-1 double mutants
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-complex-mediated transcriptional repression by enhancing DOF6 binding to the GA20ox3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Feedback control of gibberellin biosynthesis during fruit development and seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: GA signaling; degraded upon bioactive GA perception
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback from GA signaling onto GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms repressive complex with DOF6; binds promoter indirectly through DOF6
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major DELLA component for seed germination control and a representative DELLA showing direct enhancement of DOF6 repression on GA20ox3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fruits; imbibed seeds; cold-stratified seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit elongation and seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RGL2 represses GA20ox3 through DOF6 and thereby delays germination and restricts fruit elongation; rgl2 partially suppresses ga20ox3-1 phenotypes, while della-q fully rescues due to redundancy
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stratification; paclobutrazol treatment; GA-mediated DELLA degradation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stronger DELLA repression favors dormancy and reduced growth-related reproductive traits
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing fruits and decreases in seeds with increasing cold stratification
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in FLAG-RGL2 construct
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; double mutant analysis; della-q genetic analysis; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating DELLA-mediated repression may tune dormancy and growth, but redundancy and pleiotropy warrant caution
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGL2 physically interacts with DOF6 and enhances its repression of GA20ox3. 2. RGL2 itself does not bind the GA20ox3 probe alone but strengthens DOF6 DNA binding. 3. RGL2 is part of a GA-signaling negative feedback module controlling fruit length and germination timing. 4. Functional redundancy with other DELLAs limits the effect of single-gene loss.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen as the representative DELLA because of its established major role in seed germination, but the paper shows broader redundancy among DELLAs.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-complex-mediated transcriptional repression via interaction with DOF6
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback from GA signaling onto GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with DOF6 in a repressive complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant DELLA component contributing to repression of GA20ox3 and thus lower GA-dependent fruit growth and germination
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fruits; imbibed seeds; cold-stratified seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit elongation and seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with other DELLAs to repress GA20ox3 via DOF6, limiting fruit elongation and seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic GA-growth repression likely affects multiple adaptive traits
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing fruits and decreases during seed stratification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay; genetic evidence from della-q
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not ideal as a single editing target due to redundancy and pleiotropy; module-level tuning may be preferable
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAI interacts with DOF6. 2. GAI enhances DOF6 repression of the GA20ox3 promoter in transient assays. 3. GAI contributes redundantly with other DELLAs to the GA-signaling feedback module affecting fruit length and germination timing.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-gene-specific phenotypes for GA20ox3 regulation were not dissected; role inferred from interaction and della-q evidence.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-complex-mediated transcriptional repression via interaction with DOF6
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback from GA signaling onto GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with DOF6 in a repressive complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant DELLA component contributing to lowered GA20ox3 expression and delayed GA-mediated developmental outputs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fruits; imbibed seeds; cold-stratified seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit elongation and seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with other DELLAs to repress GA20ox3 through DOF6, thereby reducing fruit length and slowing germination
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic growth repression likely accompanies altered dormancy/growth balance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing fruits and decreases during seed stratification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay; genetic evidence from della-q
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not ideal as a lone breeding target because of redundancy/pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGA physically interacts with DOF6. 2. RGA enhances DOF6-mediated repression of the GA20ox3 promoter. 3. RGA functions redundantly with other DELLAs in the module controlling fruit length and seed germination timing.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific single-mutant effects on study traits were not the main focus.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-complex-mediated transcriptional repression via interaction with DOF6
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback from GA signaling onto GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with DOF6 in a repressive complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant DELLA component supporting repression of GA20ox3 and GA-dependent developmental traits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing fruits; imbibed seeds; cold-stratified seeds
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit elongation and seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with other DELLAs to repress GA20ox3 through DOF6, reducing fruit elongation and delaying germination
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing fruits and decreases during seed stratification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; dual-luciferase assay; genetic evidence from della-q
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Module-level manipulation preferable to single-gene targeting
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGL1 physically interacts with DOF6. 2. RGL1 enhances DOF6 repression of the GA20ox3 promoter. 3. RGL1 contributes redundantly with other DELLAs to control fruit length and germination timing through GA20ox3.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as part of the DELLA quartet shown to be epistatic to GA20ox3 in the della-q analysis.
